title: Understand: plant reproductive success
description: Check your understanding of plant reproductive success in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best phrase to fill in the blank.**

>A plant with more surviving offspring likely has \_\_\_\_\_\_ compared to a plant with fewer surviving offspring.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a higher reproductive success
a lower reproductive success
an equal reproductive success

assessment_items.question_content:
Plant A has a higher reproductive success than Plant B.

**Based on this information, which statement is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Plant A has more surviving offspring than Plant B.
Plant B has more surviving offspring than Plant A.
Plant A and Plant B have the same number of surviving offspring.

assessment_items.question_content:
Plant A has more surviving offspring than Plant B.

**Based on this information, which statement is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Plant A has a higher reproductive success than Plant B.
Plant B has a higher reproductive success than Plant A.
Plant A and Plant B have the same level of reproductive success.
the transfer of pollen from male to female flower parts
the formation of seeds after fertilization
the production of pollen inside flowers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Through which process is pollen transferred from male to female flower parts?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pollination
seed formation
fertilization

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these best describes what happens during pollination?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Pollen is transferred from male to female flower parts.
Seeds begin to form inside a flower.
Pollen is produced by the male parts of a flower.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a hummingbird feeding from a cardinal flower.

[[☃ image 1]]

Hummingbirds pollinate many types of plants, including the cardinal flower plant shown above.

**Which sentence best describes how pollinators, such as hummingbirds, help increase a plant’s reproductive success?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Ruby-throated hummingbird on cardinal flower](https://www.flickr.com/photos/usfwshq/5871175853/)" by Bill Buchanan/U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, [CC BY 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A hummingbird feeding from a red flower.

choices.content: Pollinators use a plant’s nectar as an energy source.
Pollinators transfer a plant’s pollen, which can lead to fertilization.
Pollinators disperse a plant’s seeds.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a moth feeding from an orchid flower.

[[☃ image 1]]

Moths pollinate many types of plants, including the orchid plant shown above.

**Which sentence best describes how pollinators, such as moths, help increase a plant’s reproductive success?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Burnet moth on orchid](https://www.flickr.com/photos/78038683@N05/6843949604)" by lee roberts, [CC BY-SA 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/)

options.alt: A moth feeding from an orchid flower.

choices.content: Pollinators use a plant’s nectar to get energy.
Pollinators make fertilization possible by transferring the plant’s pollen.
Pollinators help move seeds away from a parent plant.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows a butterfly feeding from a gayfeather flower.

[[☃ image 1]]

Butterflies pollinate many types of plants, including the gayfeather plant shown above.

**Which sentence best describes how pollinators, such as butterflies, help increase a plant’s reproductive success?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[A butterfly pollinating gayfeather blooms](https://www.flickr.com/photos/139446213@N03/24993052022/)" by USDA NRCS Texas, [public domain](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Commons:Licensing#Material_in_the_public_domain)

options.alt: A butterfly feeding from a gayfeather flower.

choices.content: Pollinators use a plant’s nectar as a source of energy.
Pollinators transfer a plant’s pollen from male to female flower parts, leading to fertilization.
Pollinators help disperse seeds away from the parent plant.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which sentence best describes how plants benefit from seed dispersal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Seed dispersal leads directly to fertilization and the production of new offspring.
Seed dispersal makes it more likely that a seed will grow in an area with plenty of resources.
Seed dispersal provides animals with an important source of food.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which sentence best describes how plants benefit from seed dispersal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Seed dispersal allows seeds to grow far from the parent plant where more resources are available.
Seed dispersal leads to fertilization and new seed formation.
Seed dispersal provides an important food source for animals.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which sentence best describes how plants benefit from seed dispersal?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Seed dispersal allows seeds to grow far from the parent plant, increasing reproductive success.
Seed dispersal leads to fertilization and the formation of new seeds.
Seed dispersal helps animals survive by providing them with a food source.
